seoTitle: How to Create a Slack User Group Step by Step
metaDescription: Learn how to build a Slack user group with a mentionable handle so you can ping an entire department at once instead of tagging people individually.
intro: Tagging ten different teammates one by one every time you need their attention gets old fast. This guide explains how to set up a Slack user group with a short mentionable handle, like @marketing-team, so you can reach a whole department in one message. You'll also learn how to control who can edit the group and how to set default channels new members automatically join.

steps.heading: Open Directories from the More menu
steps.body: Click the 'More' option in your left sidebar and select Directories to access the tools for managing people and groups.

steps.heading: Switch to User Groups and start a new group
steps.body: Swap from the People tab to User Groups, then click the 'Create User Group' button to begin the setup.

steps.heading: Name your group and set a mention handle
steps.body: Give the group a clear name and choose a short handle prefixed with @, such as @devs, that teammates can type to reach the whole group at once.

steps.heading: Add a purpose and default channels
steps.body: Fill in a short description of the group's purpose and set default channels so new members are automatically added when they join the group.

steps.heading: Add members and finish creating the group
steps.body: Search the directory for the teammates you want included, then click 'Create Group' to register the handle across the workspace.

steps.heading: Test the group and adjust membership as needed
steps.body: Post a message in a channel using the new handle to confirm it works, and use the admin panel later to add, remove, or swap members.

faq.question: What's the difference between a user group and a group DM?
faq.answer: A group DM is capped at nine people and works like a private chat, while a user group is a scalable mention handle that can include far more members and is used mainly for pinging people in channels.

faq.question: Can I change who's in a user group later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add, remove, or swap members from the group at any time through the admin panel, depending on your permission settings.

faq.question: Who can create a user group in Slack?
faq.answer: Availability depends on your workspace's admin settings and plan, since some workspaces restrict user group creation to admins only.

faq.question: Does mentioning a user group notify everyone in it?
faq.answer: Yes, typing the group's handle in a message notifies every member of that group, similar to tagging each person individually.
